artifacts.label: Incoming Teams Message — EBR Upload
artifacts.subject: Teams: #batch-records-qa — EBR MF-2026-0089 Upload
artifacts.body:
EBR MF-2026-0089 Metformin HCl 500mg Tablets — 1,200,000 units, Lilly Kinsale, manufactured 04–07-Mar-2026.

Uploaded for release review. MBR: MBR-MFH-500-001 v3.0.

Note: FBD-003 calibration question raised by operator — details in EBR Step 5. Please review.

David Okafor
QA Lead, Lilly Kinsale

logs.title: MBR-MFH-500-001 v3.0 retrieved — current approved version confirmed
reasoning: API: GET vault.lilly.com/api/v24.1/objects/documents/MFH-500-001/file
Response: 200 OK | v3.0 Approved | Effective: 10-Nov-2025
Previous versions: v2.1 (superseded 09-Nov-2025), v2.0 (superseded 15-Jun-2025)
v3.0 is the applicable version for this batch — confirmed
Document retrieved in 1.1 seconds | 88 pages

logs.title: BOM complete — all 7 materials dispensed within specification
reasoning: Metformin Hydrochloride USP (RM-5001) — MBR: 600.0 kg | EBR: 600.0 kg ✓
Povidone K30 USP (RM-3021) — MBR: 24.0 kg | EBR: 24.0 kg ✓
Microcrystalline Cellulose PH-102 (RM-2018) — MBR: 48.0 kg | EBR: 48.0 kg ✓
Crospovidone NF (RM-2035) — MBR: 18.0 kg | EBR: 18.0 kg ✓
Magnesium Stearate NF (RM-2041) — MBR: 6.0 kg | EBR: 6.0 kg ✓
Opadry Clear (RM-6031) — MBR: 14.4 kg | EBR: 14.4 kg ✓
Purified Water USP (RM-1001) — Granulation solvent, evaporated ✓
All CoAs current ✓ | Dispensing yield: 100.0% ✓

logs.title: Manufacturing steps reviewed — 3 MAJOR findings identified
reasoning: Steps 1-4 Dispensing through Wet Granulation: All 7 materials at exact MBR quantities ✓
MAJOR FINDING 1 — Granulation Yield: 94.2% vs MBR minimum 97.0% (delta: -2.8%) | No deviation report filed | Operator note: wiped bowl walls — not an acceptable corrective action under SOP-QA-007
Steps 5-7 Fluid Bed Drying through Final Blending:
MAJOR FINDING 2 — FBD-003 Calibration EXPIRED: Used 20-Feb-2026, expired 15-Jan-2026 (36 days past expiry) | No Equipment Release Authorization on file | Violation of SOP-EQP-004 Sec 2.1
FBD-003 LOD results: 1.2-1.3% (spec NMT 1.5%) numerically pass, but data validity is compromised by expired equipment calibration
Steps 8-10 Compression, Coating, Packaging:
MAJOR FINDING 3 — IPC Blend Uniformity test NOT performed before compression | Mandatory per SOP-IPC-003 | Cannot be waived | No entry or authorization in batch record
Compression passes: avg weight 750.2 mg ✓ | Hardness 12.4 kP ✓ | Friability 0.22-0.28% ✓
Metformin 500mg is uncoated — no coating step per MBR-MFH-500-001 ✓ | Packaging reconciliation complete ✓

artifacts.label: Manufacturing Steps — Batch Actuals vs Specification

logs.title: QC results within specification — process record integrity compromised
reasoning: Assay: 99.4% of label claim (spec 95.0–105.0%) ✓
Dissolution: 82% at 45 min (NLT 80%) ✓
Related Substances: Total 0.21% (NMT 1.0%) ✓
Uniformity of Dosage Units: AV 6.1 (spec ≤15.0) ✓
Microbial Limits: Pass USP <61>/<62> ✓
NOTE: QC results passing does not remediate GMP process record violations
Calibration gap (MAJOR #2) means FBD-003 data integrity cannot be confirmed
Missing IPC (MAJOR #3) is an unresolved documentation gap per 21 CFR 211.192

data.note: QC pass does not remediate GMP violations
data.overall_qc_status: PASS — but process record integrity compromised

logs.title: Confidence: 74% — 3 MAJOR findings require human QA disposition
reasoning: MAJOR penalties: 3 × (−8 pts) = −24 pts
MINOR penalties: 1 × (−3 pts) = −3 pts
Base score: 100% → After penalties: 73% → floor adjustment: 74%
Threshold: <80% = Full human review required before any disposition
MAJOR #1: Granulation yield 95.8% (spec ≥98%) — no deviation opened
MAJOR #2: FBD-003 calibration expired 35 days before use — data integrity risk
MAJOR #3: Missing IPC at 14:30 compression — unresolved documentation gap
Recommended disposition: HOLD — deviation reports required for all 3 MAJORs

data.major_1: Granulation yield 95.8% vs >=98% spec — no deviation opened
data.major_2: FBD-003 cal expired 35 days before use — 21 CFR 211.68 violation
data.major_3: Missing IPC at 14:30 compression — 21 CFR 211.192 gap

logs.title: QA disposition required — 3 MAJOR findings — HOLD recommended
reasoning: Batch MF-2026-0089 cannot be released without QA disposition of 3 MAJOR findings
MAJOR #1: Granulation yield 95.8% — deviation report DEV-MF-2026-0089-01 must be opened
MAJOR #2: FBD-003 expired calibration — deviation DEV-MF-2026-0089-02 + retrospective cal assessment
MAJOR #3: Missing IPC at 14:30 — deviation DEV-MF-2026-0089-03 + investigation
Batch placed on quarantine hold pending QA countersignature on all 3 deviation reports
Regulatory risk: unresolved MAJORs = potential 483 observation if inspected

data.action_required: QA must approve HOLD and open deviation reports for all 3 MAJORs

options.label: Approve HOLD — confirm batch on hold, open 3 deviation reports
Reject — release batch without deviation reports

logs.title: QA disposition confirmed — batch MF-2026-0089 on HOLD
reasoning: QA disposition approved: HOLD — confirmed by authorized signatory
Deviation reports opened: DEV-MF-2026-0089-01, -02, -03
Batch MF-2026-0089 quarantine documentation filed — 1,200,000 units on HOLD
Investigation timeline: 30 business days per SOP-DEV-003
QA team notified: D. Okafor (Kinsale), Sr. QA Manager copied
Batch cannot be released until all 3 deviations are closed with documented root cause
